openmeter/billing/charges/adapter.purpose: Ent/PostgreSQL adapter implementing charges.Adapter and charges.ChargesSearchAdapter. Provides all DB access for the charges domain — search queries, customer advancement candidates — with strict transaction rebinding via entutils.TransactingRepo on every method.

patterns.name: TransactingRepo on every method
patterns.description: Every exported adapter method body must be wrapped in entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx, tx *adapter) ...) so the method rebinds to the ctx-carried transaction instead of the raw client.
patterns.example: return entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a, func(ctx context.Context, tx *adapter) (T, error) { return tx.db.Entity.Query()... })

patterns.name: Tx / WithTx / Self transaction lifecycle
patterns.description: adapter implements transaction.TxCreator via Tx() (HijackTx + NewTxDriver), WithTx() (NewTxClientFromRawConfig), and Self() — this triad is required for entutils.TransactingRepo to work correctly.
patterns.example: func (a *adapter) WithTx(ctx context.Context, tx *entutils.TxDriver) *adapter { txDb := entdb.NewTxClientFromRawConfig(ctx, *tx.GetConfig()); return &adapter{db: txDb.Client(), logger: a.logger} }

patterns.name: Config.Validate() before construction
patterns.description: New(Config) calls config.Validate() and returns an error if Client or Logger is nil before constructing the adapter struct.
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (charges.Adapter, error) { if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &adapter{db: config.Client, logger: config.Logger}, nil }

patterns.name: Interface compliance assertion
patterns.description: Every interface the adapter implements has a compile-time assertion: var _ charges.ChargesSearchAdapter = (*adapter)(nil).

patterns.name: Manual pagination for GroupBy queries
patterns.description: Ent's Paginate() does not work with GroupBy; ListCustomersToAdvance applies Go-side slice pagination after fetching all grouped results with Scan().

key_file_guides.role: Defines Config, New constructor, and the adapter struct with Tx/WithTx/Self for transaction lifecycle. All query files in this package share this adapter struct.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never pass a.db directly to a helper — always go through a tx *adapter inside TransactingRepo so the Ent client is bound to the current transaction.

key_file_guides.role: Implements GetByIDs, ListCharges, and ListCustomersToAdvance using ChargesSearchV1 Ent view. Shows namespace-filter pattern and the GroupBy+manual-pagination workaround.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ChargesSearchV1 is a read-only Ent view — never attempt writes through it. GroupBy results cannot use query.Paginate(); slice manually.

key_file_guides.role: Integration tests for the adapter that run against a real Postgres instance using testutils.InitPostgresDB and migrate.Up(). SetupSuite wires a real DB; each test uses isolated namespaces.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Tests use context.Background() directly (no testing.T available at DB level) — for new tests prefer t.Context() where t is available.

anti_patterns: Adding a method that reads or writes via a.db without wrapping in entutils.TransactingRepo — breaks ctx-carried transaction isolation
Writing to ChargesSearchV1 — it is a read-only view backed by the underlying charge tables
Using query.Paginate() after GroupBy — Ent does not support this; paginate manually in Go
Constructing the adapter without calling config.Validate() — nil client causes a panic at query time
Adding business logic to adapter methods — adapters must be pure data-access; orchestration belongs in charges/service

decisions.decision: entutils.TransactingRepo wraps every method body rather than wrapping at the service call site
decisions.rationale: Charge advancement mixes reads and writes across multiple adapter calls in a single transaction; each helper must independently rebind to the ctx-carried tx to avoid partial writes.

decisions.decision: Manual Go-side pagination for ListCustomersToAdvance instead of Ent Paginate
decisions.rationale: Ent's generated Paginate() is incompatible with GROUP BY queries; the total count is computed from the full scan and sliced in Go.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new query method that must respect transactions

openmeter/billing/charges/service.purpose: Orchestration layer for the charges domain: implements charges.Service by routing to sub-services (flatfee, usagebased, creditpurchase). Owns the Create/AdvanceCharges/ApplyPatches lifecycle, auto-advancement of credit-only charges, and gathering-line creation for invoiceable charges.

patterns.name: transaction.Run wraps all multi-write operations
patterns.description: Create, AdvanceCharges, and HandleCreditPurchaseExternalPaymentStateTransition all wrap their DB-touching body in transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx) ...) to ensure atomicity.
patterns.example: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context) (charges.Charges, error) { ... })

patterns.name: Delegate per-type work to sub-services
patterns.description: service.Create calls s.flatFeeService.Create, s.usageBasedService.Create, and s.creditPurchaseService.Create separately in a single transaction; results are mapped back to original intent indexes via charges.WithIndex[T].
patterns.example:
flatFees, err := s.flatFeeService.Create(ctx, flatfee.CreateInput{...})
usageBasedCharges, err := s.usageBasedService.Create(ctx, usagebased.CreateInput{...})

patterns.name: Auto-advance credit-only charges post-create
patterns.description: After the create transaction commits, autoAdvanceCreatedCharges identifies customers with newly created credit-only charges and calls AdvanceCharges in a separate transaction so creation state is persisted even if advancement fails.

patterns.name: invokeInvoiceNowOnCreate for bypassed collection alignment
patterns.description: Credit purchases that bypass collection alignment call s.billingService.InvoicePendingLines with billing.WithBypassCollectionAlignment() after the main TX to trigger immediate invoicing.
patterns.example: s.billingService.InvoicePendingLines(ctx, billing.InvoicePendingLinesInput{..., IncludePendingLines: mo.Some(lines), AsOf: lo.ToPtr(clock.Now())}, billing.WithBypassCollectionAlignment())

patterns.name: validateNamespaceLockdown before every write operation
patterns.description: Create and AdvanceCharges both call s.validateNamespaceLockdown(namespace) before any DB work; this is mandatory for all state-changing service methods.
patterns.example: if err := s.validateNamespaceLockdown(input.Namespace); err != nil { return nil, err }

key_file_guides.role: Defines the service struct, Config, New constructor, and dependency validation. Config holds all sub-service and adapter dependencies injected at construction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All dependencies (FlatFeeService, UsageBasedService, CreditPurchaseService, BillingService, FeatureService, MetaAdapter, Adapter) must be non-nil — Config.Validate() enforces this.

key_file_guides.role: Implements charges.Service.Create — fans out to sub-services, collects gathering lines for billing, and post-commit triggers auto-advance and optional invoice-now.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The two-phase design (TX for creation + separate TX for auto-advance) is intentional; do not merge them into one transaction or crash-recovery will lose the created state.

key_file_guides.role: Implements charges.Service.AdvanceCharges — wraps in transaction.Run, lists non-final charges, dispatches to flatFeeService.AdvanceCharge or usageBasedService.AdvanceCharge by type.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Only CreditOnlySettlementMode flat fees are advanced here; non-credit-only flat fees are skipped intentionally.

key_file_guides.role: Implements HandleCreditPurchaseExternalPaymentStateTransition — type-asserts the charge to creditpurchase.Charge and dispatches to HandleExternalPaymentAuthorized or HandleExternalPaymentSettled.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Must run inside transaction.Run so the state-machine transition and DB write are atomic.

key_file_guides.role: Shared BaseSuite that wires the full charges stack (all sub-adapters, sub-services, line engines) atop billingtest.BaseSuite. All service tests embed this.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Line engines must be registered on s.BillingService before tests run — omitting RegisterLineEngine causes silent no-ops during invoice processing.

key_file_guides.role: Contains chargesByType classifier and other shared helpers used across create.go, advance.go, and patch.go.
key_file_guides.watch_for: chargesByType panics on unknown charge types — new charge types must be added here.

anti_patterns: Calling sub-service Create/Advance methods outside a transaction.Run — breaks atomicity across charge types
Merging the create transaction and the auto-advance transaction — creation state would be lost if advancement panics mid-flight
Adding billing-logic (e.g., gathering-line creation) directly to adapter methods — all orchestration belongs in service
Calling s.billingService methods without a prior validateNamespaceLockdown — bypasses namespace-level write protection
Skipping RegisterLineEngine for a new charge type's engine — the billing service will silently produce no-op lines

decisions.decision: Two separate transactions for Create: one for creation, one for auto-advance
decisions.rationale: The balance-worker can retry advancement independently; losing the creation write due to an advance panic would be unrecoverable. Separation makes both steps individually durable.

decisions.decision: Service delegates per-type work to flatfee/usagebased/creditpurchase sub-services rather than handling inline
decisions.rationale: Each charge type has distinct state machines, realization logic, and handler callbacks; a single service method would become unmanageable. Delegation keeps each type's logic cohesive.

code_examples.scenario: Implementing a new top-level charges.Service method that spans multiple charge types

openmeter/billing/charges/testutils.purpose: Shared test infrastructure for the charges domain: provides MockHandlers (no-op implementations of flatfee.Handler, creditpurchase.Handler, usagebased.Handler) and a NewServices factory that wires the full charges stack from raw dependencies for use in integration tests.

patterns.name: MockHandlers as zero-side-effect handler impls
patterns.description: mockFlatFeeHandler, mockCreditPurchaseHandler, and mockUsageBasedHandler implement all handler interface methods; ledger callbacks return a fresh ulid-based ledgertransaction.GroupReference so tests can verify IDs without a real ledger.

patterns.name: NewServices wires the full charges stack
patterns.description: NewServices(t, Config) constructs meta adapter, locker, lineage adapter/service, each charge-type adapter/service, line engines, and the top-level charges.Service in one call — mirrors production wiring in app/common without importing it.
patterns.example:
svcs, err := testutils.NewServices(t, testutils.Config{
	Client:              dbClient,
	BillingService:      billingSvc,
	FeatureService:      featureSvc,
	StreamingConnector:  mockConnector,
	FlatFeeHandler:      handlers.FlatFee,
	CreditPurchaseHandler: handlers.CreditPurchase,
	UsageBasedHandler:   handlers.UsageBased,
})

patterns.name: Config.Validate() guards all required dependencies
patterns.description: Config.Validate() returns joined errors for any nil required field (Client, BillingService, FeatureService, StreamingConnector, and all three handlers); callers must handle the error before using Services.
patterns.example: if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

key_file_guides.role: Mock implementations of flatfee.Handler, creditpurchase.Handler, usagebased.Handler. Each callback returns a deterministic ledgertransaction.GroupReference via newMockLedgerTransactionGroupReference().
key_file_guides.watch_for: When a new handler method is added to any Handler interface, a corresponding no-op must be added here or the mock will stop compiling.

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, Validate(), Services struct, and NewServices factory function that builds the full charges sub-service tree for tests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: NewServices registers flat-fee and usage-based line engines on BillingService via RegisterLineEngine — if BillingService does not support RegisterLineEngine, construction will fail.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common from testutils — creates import cycles; build all dependencies from package constructors directly
Adding stateful side effects to mock handlers without a Reset() method — test pollution across suite test cases
Using NewServices in production wiring (app/common) — this factory is test-only and uses mock handlers

decisions.decision: MockHandlers returns a new ulid on every ledger callback instead of a fixed constant
decisions.rationale: Tests that verify ledger transaction group IDs need unique, distinguishable IDs per callback invocation; a fixed constant would make it impossible to differentiate which callback ran.
